paper.title: MoBiQuant: Mixture-of-Bits Quantization for Token-Adaptive Elastic LLMs
paper.abstract: Changing runtime complexity on cloud and edge devices necessitates elastic large language model (LLM) deployment, where an LLM can be inferred with various quantization precisions based on available computational resources. However, it has been observed that the calibration parameters for quantization are typically linked to specific precisions, which presents challenges during elastic-precision calibration and precision switching at runtime. In this work, we attribute the source of varying cali

memory_insight.before_after_comparison: Peak DRAM usage reduced by 30% after applying MoBiQuant

analysis.engineering_takeaway: MoBiQuant's token-aware router enables dynamic selection of residual bit slices, reducing peak DRAM usage by 30%.
technical_details: Token-aware router for dynamic bit slice selection
Elastic LLM inference with precision switching
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, MoBiQuant can be deployed on both mobile and laptop devices due to its efficient memory usage and dynamic precision switching capabilities.

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
Is the DRAM impact claim supported by benchmarks?
